question_text: The Netaji Subhash National Institute of Sports (NSNIS), which is the premier sports coaching institute of India, is located in which of the following cities?

options.text: New Delhi
Patiala, Punjab
Gwalior, Madhya Pradesh
Bengaluru, Karnataka

explanation: The Netaji Subhash National Institute of Sports (NSNIS) is located in Patiala, Punjab. Established in 1961, it is India's premier institute for training coaches and sports scientists. It functions under the Sports Authority of India (SAI).
elimination_hints: Gwalior is the location of LNUPE; Bengaluru has a SAI centre but not the main NSNIS. Patiala is the well-known answer.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Which of the following correctly describes the primary function of the Sports Authority of India (SAI) in the development of sports infrastructure and talent in the country?

options.text: SAI is a private company that organises commercial cricket tournaments for entertainment purposes only

SAI is an autonomous body under the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports that manages sports infrastructure, runs training centres, identifies and nurtures sporting talent, and implements government schemes for sports development
SAI is an international organisation headquartered in Geneva that governs Olympic sports worldwide
SAI is responsible only for manufacturing sports equipment and has no role in athlete training

explanation: The Sports Authority of India (SAI) is an autonomous body under the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports, Government of India. Its functions include managing sports infrastructure and stadia, running regional and sub-centres for training, identifying and nurturing sporting talent through schemes like the National Sports Talent Contest, and implementing government sports development programmes.
elimination_hints: A describes a private entity; C describes the IOC; D limits SAI to equipment manufacturing. B correctly captures SAI's comprehensive mandate.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Which of the following career options in Physical Education specifically involves analysing athletic performance using video technology, motion capture data and biomechanical principles to improve technique?

options.text: Sports journalist who writes match reports for newspapers and magazines
Sports biomechanist or performance analyst who uses scientific tools to evaluate and optimise athlete movement patterns
Sports equipment manufacturer who designs and produces cricket bats and footballs
Sports event coordinator who manages logistics of tournaments and competitions

explanation: A sports biomechanist or performance analyst uses video analysis, motion capture technology, force platforms and biomechanical principles to analyse athletic movement patterns. They provide evidence-based recommendations to coaches and athletes for technique improvement and injury prevention — a specialised career in sports science.
elimination_hints: A involves writing, not analysis; C involves manufacturing; D involves logistics. Only B involves scientific analysis of movement.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Analyze how the role of media and corporate sponsors has transformed the landscape of professional sports careers in India over the past two decades, particularly in terms of financial viability and public recognition.

options.text: Media and sponsors have had no impact on sports careers in India and athletes still rely entirely on government support

Television broadcasting, digital media coverage and corporate sponsorship have created substantial revenue streams, making professional sports careers financially viable, increasing public recognition of athletes, and generating new career opportunities in sports marketing, broadcasting, management and content creation
Media involvement has only benefited cricket and has had no impact on any other sport in India
Corporate sponsors have reduced the number of career options in sports by replacing all volunteer positions with paid roles

explanation: Media and corporate sponsorship have transformed Indian sports: TV broadcasting rights, digital streaming, brand endorsements and league sponsorships have created significant revenue. This has made careers in sports financially sustainable, elevated athlete recognition, and spawned new career paths in sports broadcasting, marketing, management, social media content creation and event management.
elimination_hints: A ignores the documented commercial transformation; C overlooks impact on kabaddi, badminton, football etc.; D makes an illogical claim.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: The International Olympic Committee (IOC), which governs the Olympic Movement and oversees the organisation of the Olympic Games, has its headquarters located in which city?

options.text: Paris, France
Lausanne, Switzerland
New York, United States
Tokyo, Japan

explanation: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) is headquartered in Lausanne, Switzerland. Founded in 1894 by Pierre de Coubertin, the IOC is the supreme authority governing the Olympic Movement, overseeing the organisation of Summer and Winter Olympic Games.
elimination_hints: Paris hosted the 1900 and 2024 Olympics but is not the IOC HQ. The IOC has been in Lausanne since 1915.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Which of the following correctly identifies the role and function of the Indian Olympic Association (IOA) in the context of Indian sports administration?

options.text: IOA is a commercial franchise that owns and operates professional sports leagues in India

IOA is the national Olympic committee of India that selects and sends Indian contingents to the Olympic Games, Asian Games and Commonwealth Games, and oversees the functioning of national sports federations
IOA is a government ministry directly controlled by the Prime Minister's Office
IOA is responsible only for organising the Indian Premier League cricket tournament

explanation: The Indian Olympic Association (IOA) is the national Olympic committee of India, affiliated with the IOC. It selects and manages Indian contingents for the Olympics, Asian Games, Commonwealth Games and other multi-sport events. It also oversees the functioning of national sports federations affiliated with it.
elimination_hints: A describes a commercial entity; C wrongly places it under PMO (it is autonomous); D confuses it with BCCI/IPL.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Analyze why the career of a 'sports psychologist' has become increasingly important in modern competitive sports, and identify which of the following best describes their primary professional role.

options.text: A sports psychologist treats only physical injuries sustained by athletes during training and competition

A sports psychologist works with athletes to enhance mental skills such as focus, confidence, stress management, motivation and team cohesion, and helps them cope with performance anxiety and competitive pressure
A sports psychologist is responsible for designing training programmes related to physical fitness and conditioning
A sports psychologist organises sports events and manages tournament schedules for national federations

explanation: A sports psychologist specialises in the mental and emotional aspects of athletic performance. Their role includes enhancing mental skills (concentration, visualisation, confidence), managing performance anxiety and stress, building motivation and team cohesion, and supporting athlete well-being. As competition has intensified, the mental dimension has become a recognised performance differentiator.
elimination_hints: A describes a physiotherapist; C describes a strength and conditioning coach; D describes an event manager.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Evaluate the contribution of the Lakshmibai National University of Physical Education (LNUPE), Gwalior, to the development of Physical Education as a professional discipline in India since its establishment.

options.text: LNUPE has made no significant contribution and is primarily a school for recreational activities

LNUPE has been a pioneering institution that offers undergraduate, postgraduate and doctoral programmes in Physical Education, produces trained PE teachers, coaches and researchers, and has contributed to establishing PE as a recognised academic and professional discipline in India
LNUPE only offers short-term certificate courses and does not grant any university degrees
LNUPE focuses exclusively on yoga and has no programmes related to sports science or Physical Education

explanation: LNUPE (established 1957, named after Rani Lakshmibai) is India's premier university for Physical Education. It offers BPEd, MPEd and PhD programmes, trains PE teachers and coaches, conducts sports science research, and has been instrumental in establishing PE as a recognised academic discipline with professional career pathways in India.
elimination_hints: A trivialises a premier university; C is factually wrong (it grants degrees); D ignores its broad sports science portfolio.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Which of the following is NOT a recognised career option available to graduates of Physical Education programmes in India?

options.text: Physical Education teacher in schools and colleges
Sports coach or personal fitness trainer in gymnasiums
Chartered Accountant specialising in financial auditing and taxation
Sports journalist, commentator or sports broadcaster

explanation: Chartered Accountancy is a finance and accounting profession requiring CA qualification and is not a career in Physical Education. All other options — PE teacher, sports coach, fitness trainer, sports journalist, commentator and broadcaster — are recognised career paths for PE graduates.
elimination_hints: A, B and D are directly related to sports and physical education. C (Chartered Accountant) belongs to finance/accounting and has no connection to PE.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Evaluate the growing importance of 'sports management' as a career field in India, considering the expansion of professional leagues, large-scale sporting events and the increasing commercialisation of sports.

options.text: Sports management has no future in India because sports events are always organised by volunteers without any professional management

Sports management has become a vital career field because the growth of professional leagues, international events hosted by India, corporate sponsorships and athlete brand management require professionally trained managers in event operations, marketing, finance, legal compliance and facility management
Sports management is limited to managing cricket teams only and has no application in other sports
Sports management is declining as a career because automation has replaced all management functions in the sports industry

explanation: The rapid growth of professional leagues (IPL, PKL, ISL), India hosting international events, increasing corporate sponsorship, athlete brand management and sports facility development have created strong demand for professionally trained sports managers. The field encompasses event operations, marketing, finance, legal affairs, facility management and athlete management.
elimination_hints: A ignores the professional management reality; C limits to cricket; D makes an unfounded claim about automation replacing management.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Match the following sports institutions in Column A with their correct location and primary function in Column B.

column_a.text: NSNIS (Netaji Subhash National Institute of Sports)

column_b.text: Gwalior — premier university offering BPEd, MPEd and PhD in Physical Education
Chennai (Nandanam) — one of the oldest PE colleges in India, affiliated to YMCA movement
Patiala — premier coaching institute for training sports coaches under SAI
Lucknow — college of Physical Education established for women's PE development

explanation: NSNIS → Patiala, coaching institute under SAI (R). LNUPE → Gwalior, PE university (P). YMCA College of PE → Chennai (Q). LCCPE → Lucknow, women's PE development (S).
elimination_hints: NSNIS-Patiala (1-R) and LNUPE-Gwalior (2-P) are the most commonly tested pairings. Use these anchors.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Match the following career roles in Physical Education and Sports in Column A with the specific professional responsibility they perform in Column B.

column_b.text: Designs and develops software applications, performance tracking tools and analytics platforms used by athletes and coaches
Plans and manages the logistics, scheduling, venue preparation and smooth execution of sports tournaments and events
Provides rehabilitation treatment for sports injuries using manual therapy, electrotherapy and exercise-based recovery programmes
Designs personalised meal plans, monitors hydration strategies and advises athletes on dietary supplements for optimal performance

explanation: Sports physiotherapist → injury rehabilitation (R). Sports nutritionist → meal plans and dietary advice (S). Sports software engineer → software and analytics platforms (P). Sports event coordinator → logistics and tournament management (Q).
elimination_hints: Physiotherapist (1) clearly relates to rehabilitation (R); nutritionist (2) clearly relates to meal plans (S). Anchor with these.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Match the following international and national sports bodies in Column A with their correct full form and primary role in Column B.

column_b.text: Indian Olympic Association — selects and manages Indian contingents for multi-sport international events
Sports Authority of India — manages sports infrastructure and implements government sports schemes
International Olympic Committee — supreme governing body of the Olympic Movement worldwide
Fédération Internationale de Football Association — international governing body of football (soccer)

explanation: IOC → International Olympic Committee, Olympic Movement (R). IOA → Indian Olympic Association, Indian contingents (P). SAI → Sports Authority of India, infrastructure and schemes (Q). FIFA → international football governing body (S).
elimination_hints: IOC (1) is clearly the international Olympic body (R); FIFA (4) is clearly football (S). These are the most well-known pairings.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Read the Assertion and Reason below and select the correct option regarding career opportunities in Physical Education.
assertion: The range of career opportunities available to Physical Education graduates has expanded significantly in the twenty-first century beyond traditional teaching and coaching roles.
reason: The growth of professional sports leagues, fitness industry, sports media, sports technology and wellness tourism has created new professional roles such as sports analyst, fitness consultant, sports marketing manager and wellness programme designer.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

explanation: The Assertion is true — PE career options have expanded greatly. The Reason is also true and correctly explains the expansion: the growth of professional leagues, fitness industry, sports media, technology and wellness tourism directly created the new roles mentioned, which expanded career options beyond teaching and coaching.
elimination_hints: Both statements are well-supported by current industry trends. The Reason lists specific industry growth areas that directly explain why new career roles have emerged.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Read the Assertion and Reason below and select the correct option regarding the NSNIS Patiala and its role in Indian sports.
assertion: The NSNIS Patiala has played a crucial role in improving the quality of sports coaching in India by producing internationally certified coaches in various Olympic disciplines.
reason: NSNIS offers only recreational fitness programmes and does not provide any specialised coaching certification or diploma courses in specific sports.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

explanation: The Assertion is true — NSNIS has significantly improved coaching quality in India by offering specialised coaching diploma programmes in various sports disciplines. However, the Reason is false — NSNIS offers specialised coaching certification and diploma courses (not just recreational programmes) in sports like athletics, swimming, hockey, football, boxing and many more Olympic disciplines.
elimination_hints: The Reason claims NSNIS offers 'only recreational fitness programmes' — this is clearly wrong as NSNIS is India's premier coaching institute with specialised sport-specific diploma courses.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Read the Assertion and Reason below and select the correct option regarding the influence of media on sports development in India.
assertion: Television and digital media have significantly increased the popularity and viewership of non-cricket sports like kabaddi, badminton and football in India.
reason: Professional leagues such as Pro Kabaddi League, Premier Badminton League and Indian Super League received extensive television and digital broadcast coverage, bringing these sports into mainstream public consciousness.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

explanation: The Assertion is true — media has boosted non-cricket sports' popularity. The Reason is also true and provides the correct explanation: the broadcast coverage of professional leagues (PKL, PBL, ISL) directly brought these sports to mainstream audiences, which is the mechanism through which media increased their popularity.
elimination_hints: Both statements are well-documented facts about Indian sports development. The Reason explains the specific mechanism (league broadcasting) behind the Assertion's claim.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Arrange the following institutions and organisations in the chronological order of their establishment in India, from the earliest to the most recent.
items: II. YMCA College of Physical Education, Chennai
IV. Lakshmibai National University of Physical Education (LNUPE), Gwalior

options.text: II → III → IV → I
III → I → II → IV
I → IV → III → II
IV → II → I → III

explanation: YMCA College Chennai (1920) → Indian Olympic Association (1927) → LNUPE Gwalior (1957, originally LNCPE) → Sports Authority of India (1984). Correct chronological order: II → III → IV → I.
elimination_hints: YMCA Chennai (II) is one of the oldest PE institutions in India (1920s); SAI (I) was established in 1984 and is the most recent. Anchor with these endpoints.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Arrange the following steps in the typical career pathway of a student who aspires to become a certified sports coach in India, from the initial step to achieving the certification.
items: I. Complete a specialised coaching diploma from NSNIS Patiala or equivalent SAI centre
II. Complete senior secondary education (Class XII) with Physical Education as a subject
III. Gain practical coaching experience through internships and assistant coaching roles
IV. Complete a Bachelor's degree in Physical Education (BPEd) or related sports science programme

options.text: II → IV → I → III
I → III → II → IV
IV → II → III → I
III → I → IV → II

explanation: Career pathway: Complete Class XII with PE (II) → Complete BPEd or sports science degree (IV) → Complete NSNIS coaching diploma (I) → Gain practical coaching experience (III). This follows the natural educational progression from school to degree to specialisation to practice.
elimination_hints: Class XII (II) is the first academic prerequisite; practical coaching experience (III) comes after formal certification. Anchor with these.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: True or False: The Sports Authority of India (SAI) functions under the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports of the Government of India and is responsible for managing major sports facilities and developing sports talent across the country.

options.text: True
False

explanation: This statement is True. SAI is an autonomous body under the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports, Government of India. It manages major sports facilities like stadia and training centres, and runs talent identification and development programmes across India.
elimination_hints: This is a standard factual statement about SAI's administrative structure and mandate — both the ministry affiliation and the dual function (facilities + talent) are correct.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE

question_text: Study the diagram below that shows career pathways available to Physical Education graduates, organised by sector (education, sports industry, health and wellness, media and technology). Based on the diagram, which sector has seen the most significant growth in new career roles over the past decade, driven by the digital revolution and commercialisation of sports in India?

options.text: Education sector — because new schools are being opened in rural areas
Health and wellness sector — because hospitals have started recruiting PE graduates as doctors

Media and technology sector — because digital broadcasting, sports analytics platforms, social media content creation and sports app development have created entirely new career roles that did not exist a decade ago
Education sector — because the number of university PE departments has doubled in the last year

explanation: The media and technology sector has seen the most significant growth in new PE-related career roles over the past decade. Digital broadcasting, OTT streaming of sports, sports analytics (data science), social media sports content creation, fitness app development and sports tech startups have created career roles that simply did not exist earlier — driven by the digital revolution and sports commercialisation.
elimination_hints: B is wrong (PE graduates are not recruited as doctors); A and D overstate education sector growth. C correctly identifies media/technology as the fastest-growing sector.
subject: Physical Education
chapter: Sociological Aspects, Training & Career in PE
